In the Supreme Court of India. Civil Appeal No. 1 of 2024. Judgment of the Court.

1. The appellant challenged the impugned order on the ground that the principles
of natural justice and a fair hearing had been violated during the proceedings.

2. The respondent contended that an adequate opportunity of hearing was in fact
afforded to the appellant at every material stage of the inquiry before the order.

3. This Court has consistently held that the rule of audi alteram partem is a
facet of Article 14 of the Constitution and applies to administrative action.

4. The doctrine of proportionality requires that any restriction imposed by the
State be the least intrusive means reasonably available to achieve its aim.

¶ 5 The Court reiterated that judicial review of administrative action remains a
basic feature of the Constitution and cannot be ousted by a privative clause.

6. In the result, the appeal is allowed and the impugned order is set aside,
with the matter remitted for a fresh hearing and no order as to costs.
